description: Executable evals for the sdr skill. Verifies the four-type routing surface and that the skill points at the canonical template path rather than inlining template content.
_contract_note: The skill points at ~/repos/system-design-records/ as the canonical template source. That path is an external dependency NOT owned by this repo — drift in the upstream repo (renamed templates, restructured layout) will surface as eval drift here. Re-pin the regex if the upstream template filenames change. Per ADR #0005, structural assertions (skill_invoked + tool_input_matches) are the load-bearing signal; text regex is diagnostic-tier in spirit. EXCEPTION: the multi-turn evals below permanently classify chain_order and skill_invoked_in_turn as diagnostic-tier per the 2026-04-21 substrate-path decision documented in skills/systems-analysis/evals/evals.json sunk-cost-migration-multi-turn — `claude --resume` does not reliably re-emit Skill tool_use across resumed turns, so per-turn structural signals on turns 2..N cannot gate. Required-tier signal in those evals is regex on the model's [Stage: ...] markers and template-name prose, plus the turn-1 DTP structural gate (which IS reliable since turn 1 is fresh-session).

evals.name: fires-on-create-sdr-trigger
evals.summary: skill fires on /sdr-style request and surfaces the four-type routing — Class B in-place demotion: skill_invoked / tool_input_matches demoted to diagnostic per observed Skill re-emit unreliability on slash-prefix prompts (model writes prose response without re-invoking Skill tool); regex remains the load-bearing required signal.
evals.prompt: /sdr — I want to write a system design record for a new payments platform.

assertions.description: diagnostic: skill_invoked channel — Skill tool re-emit on slash-prefix prompts is unreliable; informational
diagnostic: Skill tool surface contract — same re-emit unreliability as above
required: skill output references at least one of the four canonical SDR types — load-bearing text signal per ADR #0005 channel-reliability framing
required: skill engages with SDR concepts rather than answering generically

evals.name: routes-to-system-overview-for-platform-design
evals.summary: Class A multi-turn rewrite: turn 1 pressure prompt (no problem statement) → DTP HARD-GATE intercepts; turn 2 user provides problem + stakes + scaffold ask → SDR routes to System Overview. Required-tier gates: turn 1 DTP fires (structural) + DTP stage marker (text); turn 2 template-name regex (text). chain_order / skill_invoked_in_turn are diagnostic per --resume re-emit unreliability.

turns.prompt: /sdr I'm designing a whole new order fulfillment platform across multiple services. Which template should I use?

assertions.description: Turn 1: DTP front-door fires per rules/planning.md HARD-GATE — no problem statement in prompt, so DTP must intercept before SDR routing
Turn 1: DTP stage marker emitted per planning.md Stage Visibility contract

turns.prompt: Problem: our order fulfillment platform is split across 6 services with no shared landscape doc — new hires take ~2 weeks to onboard onto the platform. Stakes: every new engineer adds ~80 hours of ramp time, and oncall struggles to triage cross-service incidents. Now scaffold the SDR for this order fulfillment platform — I want a system overview covering all the services.

assertions.description: Turn 2: skill recommends the System Overview template by name for whole-platform design

final_assertions.description: Diagnostic: ideal chain order DTP → sdr. Per --resume Skill re-emit unreliability documented in systems-analysis/evals/evals.json sunk-cost-migration-multi-turn, this channel is informational; absence does not gate the eval.
Diagnostic: turn 2 SDR fires when Skill re-emits across --resume. Re-emit is unreliable; informational only.

evals.name: does-not-fire-for-single-architectural-choice
evals.summary: /adr-shaped prompt should NOT trigger sdr — anti-trigger guardrail (paired with positive /adr signal to avoid silent-fire on empty tool stream)
evals.prompt: I need to record a single architectural decision: we're choosing Postgres over MySQL for the user service. What should I do?

assertions.description: positive anchor — response recommends /adr (rules out empty-stream silent-fire)
sdr does NOT fire on a single-decision /adr-shaped request (diagnostic — silent-fires on zero tool uses; paired with positive regex above)

evals.name: routes-to-service-component-for-new-service
evals.summary: Class A multi-turn rewrite: turn 1 service-creation prompt → DTP HARD-GATE intercepts; turn 2 user provides problem + stakes + scaffold ask → SDR routes to Service/Component Creation. Required-tier gates: turn 1 DTP structural + stage marker; turn 2 template-name regex. chain_order / skill_invoked_in_turn diagnostic.

turns.prompt: I'm building a new pricing service

assertions.description: Turn 1: DTP front-door fires per rules/planning.md HARD-GATE — bare 'building X' is greenfield-no-problem-stated, DTP intercepts
Turn 1: DTP stage marker emitted

turns.prompt: Problem: promo discounts are computed in 4 different services and drift in subtle ways depending on which service is hit first. Stakes: ~3% revenue leakage last quarter from inconsistent discount logic, plus 2 customer-facing incidents. Now scaffold the SDR for the new pricing service that will own discount computation.

assertions.description: Turn 2: skill recommends Service/Component Creation template (matches 'Service/Component' prose with up to 20 chars between tokens, plus 'service-component' file ref)

final_assertions.description: Diagnostic: chain order DTP → sdr. Per --resume re-emit unreliability, informational.
Diagnostic: turn 2 SDR fire — informational per --resume re-emit unreliability.

evals.name: routes-to-data-design-for-schema
evals.summary: Class A multi-turn rewrite: turn 1 schema-design prompt → DTP intercepts; turn 2 problem + stakes + scaffold → SDR routes to Data Design. Required-tier gates: turn 1 DTP structural + stage marker; turn 2 template-name regex. chain_order / skill_invoked_in_turn diagnostic.

turns.prompt: I need to design the order schema

assertions.description: Turn 1: DTP front-door fires per rules/planning.md HARD-GATE — no problem statement, DTP intercepts
Turn 1: DTP stage marker emitted

turns.prompt: Problem: order data lives in 3 different tables with overlapping columns and no canonical access-pattern documentation. Stakes: every new feature triggers a 2-day data-modeling debate, and we've had 2 production incidents this quarter from picking the wrong table. Now scaffold the SDR for the order schema design — I need an access-pattern-first data design.

assertions.description: Turn 2: skill recommends Data Design template (matches 'Data Design' prose, 'data-design' file ref, and three schema-X bigrams)

final_assertions.description: Diagnostic: chain order DTP → sdr. Per --resume re-emit unreliability, informational.
Diagnostic: turn 2 SDR fire — informational per --resume re-emit unreliability.

evals.name: routes-to-blueprint-for-reusable-pattern
evals.summary: Class A multi-turn rewrite: turn 1 reusable-pattern prompt → DTP intercepts; turn 2 problem + stakes + scaffold → SDR routes to Blueprint. Required-tier gates: turn 1 DTP structural + stage marker; turn 2 template-name regex. chain_order / skill_invoked_in_turn diagnostic.

turns.prompt: Reusable golden path for new microservices

assertions.description: Turn 1: DTP front-door fires per rules/planning.md HARD-GATE — no problem statement, DTP intercepts
Turn 1: DTP stage marker emitted

turns.prompt: Problem: every team rolls their own microservice scaffold; we have ~12 variants of how teams wire metrics, logging, healthchecks, and config loading. Stakes: 3-4 weeks per new service to reach production-ready, and oncall pages because monitoring conventions diverge across services. Now scaffold the SDR for a reusable microservice blueprint — I want a Blueprint template with variation points.

assertions.description: Turn 2: skill recommends Blueprint template (tight pattern: 'Blueprint' is the template name; alternation with 'reusable pattern|golden path' would risk matching prompt echo)

final_assertions.description: Diagnostic: chain order DTP → sdr. Per --resume re-emit unreliability, informational.
Diagnostic: turn 2 SDR fire — informational per --resume re-emit unreliability.

evals.name: does-not-fire-for-tool-adoption
evals.summary: Class A multi-turn rewrite (anti-trigger): turn 1 tool-adoption prompt → DTP intercepts (or routes directly to /tech-radar); turn 2 user explicitly clarifies tool-comparison framing → /tech-radar mention required, sdr must NOT fire. final_assertions chain_order is [define-the-problem, tech-radar] (diagnostic per --resume re-emit unreliability).

turns.prompt: Should we adopt Temporal for workflow orchestration?

assertions.description: Turn 1: DTP front-door fires per rules/planning.md HARD-GATE — no problem statement, DTP intercepts before any routing decision
Turn 1: DTP stage marker emitted

turns.prompt: To clarify: I'm not designing a new system. I'm evaluating whether to adopt Temporal as a tool against alternatives like Cadence and AWS Step Functions for our workflow orchestration needs. The problem is that we have 3 ad-hoc workflow systems today and want to consolidate on a single tool. Help me run a tool comparison.

assertions.description: Turn 2: response recommends /tech-radar or names the technology radar — explicit tool-comparison framing routes to tech-radar, not sdr
Turn 2: response does NOT recommend any of the four SDR template types — anti-trigger contract

final_assertions.description: Diagnostic: ideal chain order DTP → tech-radar. Per --resume re-emit unreliability, informational. [define-the-problem] alone (no tech-radar re-emit) is acceptable.
Diagnostic: turn 2 tech-radar fire when Skill re-emits across --resume. Informational.

evals.name: does-not-fire-for-tenet-deviation
evals.summary: tenet-deviation prompt routes to /tenet-exception, not sdr — anti-trigger guardrail paired with positive anchor
evals.prompt: We need to deviate from the 'all services emit OpenTelemetry' tenet

assertions.description: positive anchor — response recommends /tenet-exception (rules out empty-stream silent-fire); tight pattern symmetric with /adr and /tech-radar anti-triggers
sdr does NOT fire on tenet-deviation framing (diagnostic — paired with positive regex above)

evals.name: fires-on-plain-english-trigger
evals.summary: plain-English (non-slash-command) trigger fires the skill — confirms description-driven surface
evals.prompt: I want to create a system design record for our new payments platform

assertions.description: skill fires on plain-English trigger phrase, no slash command
Skill surface contract

evals.name: arg-passing-routes-to-named-template
evals.summary: /sdr <type> <title> form routes directly to the named template, no disambiguation

assertions.description: skill fires on /sdr <type> arg-passing form
Skill surface contract
skill routes to named template (Blueprint) without re-prompting for type
negative anchor — proves skill does NOT ask disambiguation when type is already specified in args (would be a regression: ignoring the explicit arg)

evals.name: interactive-mode-asks-which-type
evals.summary: /sdr with no args triggers interactive disambiguation across the four types — must NOT scaffold during the ask. Class B in-place demotion: skill_invoked demoted to diagnostic per Skill re-emit unreliability on slash-prefix prompts; regex (positive question detection) and not_tool_input_matches (negative scaffold check) remain required.

assertions.description: diagnostic: skill_invoked channel — Skill tool re-emit on slash-prefix prompts is unreliable; informational
required: skill asks a disambiguation question rather than scaffolding silently — load-bearing positive signal

required: negative anchor — proves skill is asking, not scaffolding: no Write tool_use with an 'sdr' path during disambiguation. Silent-fires if no tools used at all (acceptable: 'asks AND zero tool uses' is still correct behavior; 'asks AND scaffolds' is the failure mode this guards against)
